transcript.text: This meeting is being recorded.
Hi, team.
Mansi.
Hi.
How are you all today?
Yeah, good, Bailey.
Hope you are doing good.
I'm good.
Thank you.
I'm good.
We've had some crazy weather on the east coast of the U.S.
last week.
Okay.
Hope things are fine now.
Yeah, we're, I'm all good now, but I think we've had like three feet of snow.
So it's been kind of a crazy week for a lot of people here, but all good now.
Stay safe.
Yeah, thank you.
Do you know, is Ankit coming today?
Should I jump in or should I wait a minute to see?
No, Ankit is not joining.
I think we can continue.
Okay, I will just jump right in then.
Okay.
Okay.
So I think no big, huge updates published.
We're continuing to publish the CVEs as usual.
We're creating the expansion list for 2024 and 2025.
I've asked our technical SEO experts to step in and help us create the list because I found that I felt like the data in SEMrush was missing some of the search volume there.
So hopefully we'll have a full list for that and we can start, go back and start executing on that next week.
I just wanted to make sure we weren't missing anything.
For CS101, we're all good.
We still have about 30 topics.
So I think a good runway of content there.
So foreign production, and then we have some time to kind of review some of those recommendations that I shared.
with you as well and kind of for your team to decide.
What direction we want to take to act on those.
So no big concerns there.

In terms of just the content strategy review I shared, oops, that should be, for last time, I didn't know if your team had had a chance to kind of dig into some of those recommendations and want to make sure if you had any questions or kind of any concerns that we had time to talk talk through them now.
I realize without Ankit here, if it makes more sense, we can also touch base about those next week when he's on the call as well.
So I just went through the list of the recommendation phases.
With approach and guidelines for refresh, I just wanted to confirm, will you give suggestions that we can incorporate or will you directly do those?
In that particular article.
So how is it going for the refreshes?
Yeah, no, that's okay.
So for the refreshes, I think we would take a slightly different approach.
Usually what we do, one of the reasons I asked about this is we usually create a separate pipeline to refresh articles than we do for the normal pipeline.
And the reason there is because it depends a little bit on the article itself.
But for anything that's already been indexed and is ranking, we obviously, we don't want to just toss it all out and start all over again.
The goal there would be to improve the content, to update it, to deepen the context with the idea of giving it a boost and kind of pushing it on to page one.
If it's especially content that's sort of kind of stuck at page two or three, this is often the best way to just kind of give it that push it needs to climb the rankings.
And then I think if there's content that's kind of old, that's not.
Ranking, we would sort of assess that and see if we should just kind of take a different angle on it and start over again.

But the goal here is really to kind of, for things that are already ranking or performing, to change them as little as possible in a way that's going to hurt the rankings, just to kind of improve the content, to give it that extra boost.
So, and then obviously, you know, I think if your team has specific guidelines in terms of the way you would want us to approach refreshes, we could build that into the pipeline as well.

And I think we would treat this a bit like a calibration like we did in those early articles where I would love to run, you know, maybe two and just get a sense from you and get feedback from your team on if that feels like kind of the right approach.
So we wouldn't just kind of start, jump right in.
We would want to kind of calibrate.
And make sure that you were comfortable with the approach we were taking.
Yeah, that makes sense.
I and Karthi were also discussing.
I think we can discuss more once Hunkit is there, and we can internally also review once on how we can approach for those particular articles.
Okay.
So maybe I'll plan to – we'll hold off on this for now until you sort of have a chance to discuss the approach internally, or would you prefer we run one just as a test?
Can I just discuss it with Hunkit and get back to you by tomorrow, UD?
Yes, of course.
Yeah, no problem.
Sometimes I find it's easier to, like, have an actual article to inform your discussion, but I'm also happy to just press pause until you have a chance to discuss it internally and define that.
Okay.
So I'll wait to sort of hear back from you all, but we'll go ahead and get started on just
Creating that pipeline, because that takes a bit of engineering time to do that.
So we can get started on that.
So hopefully it's ready to go once you have a chance to talk through it.
And we can kind of create separate artifacts to refine the approach there as well.
Okay.
And then for the CS101, I was wondering if you had had a chance to take a look at that list of keywords and had any kind of overall overall feedback there.
Karthi, do you have, did you get a chance to run through those keywords?
I think Mahindra is, will be having more clarity on this.
I will get back to you on this, Bailey.
Okay.
Sounds good.
Like I said, we have some articles in the backlog to run on.
So this is really just kind of for planning what our next stage is.
For the Threat Actor Database, I know Ankit asked to pull together some comp examples.
So I'm pulling those together.
Our technical SEO expert who did the research was out of office.
So I asked him yesterday to see if he had some good comps.
So I will share those as soon as I have those today.
I'll just put them in Slack.
And then I know that there was going to also be some kind of conversations gauging the internal appetites to do that too.
So I'll share those.
But I'll just assume that we'll kind of circle back on that when you've had a chance to talk about that internally.
Okay.
think that was – go ahead.
I was asking when will we get the comps.
Take examples for the Threat Actor Database.
I'm hoping that – You're dropping too, I'm I'm – Thank
I was hoping to just drop it on Slack today.
Okay, got it.
So I'll share those on Slack today.

And then I know we talked for the GA4 event mapping, we sort of talked about the most kind of important event yesterday, but I think it would probably still be helpful to sort of have a mapping to the full list.
As we kind of, now that we have this custom report, as we start kind of sharing those findings, it would be useful to have an understanding, I think, of what those events map to.
So I think when you get a chance, it would be helpful for your team to share that.
Yeah, either Ankit will be, mostly Ankit will be sharing you with the GA4 events.
Okay, perfect.
Thank you.
Okay, and then for check that, I did add your team.
you.
you.
You're Thank
So you should have a link in your inbox, your email inbox, which would allow you to connect to the SentinelOne account and check that our AI visibility tool.
So when you get a chance, it's a new tool and there have been a few bugs I found with people getting their account set up.
So if you have any issues, just let me know on Slack and I'll look into it.
Hopefully it all goes smoothly.
And if there was anyone else, I added the three of you, but if there's anyone else who needs access, let me know and I can add them from your team as well.
Yeah, Mahendra will need access to us.
Mahendra will need access.
Okay, perfect.
I'll add.
I also got checked the inbox and I got a mail.
So you have invited me to the tool, access the tool.
But when I accept the invite and logged in, tried to log in and asked me for an OK email and I gave my...
Email ID and it said couldn't find your account.
Is it something which I need to sign up or just log in?
You should be able to.
The link should take you somewhere where you can just accept the invitation.
So let me look into that.
That's the bug that I think has been occurring where it's not connecting directly to your account.
So let me look into that and I'll touch base with our tech team and get back to you and see if there's anything else you need to do or if they just need to fix it on our side.
So sorry about that.
No issues.
Okay.
Then in terms of reporting, so we'll do the full, you know, February performance reporting next week.
And just when we have the opportunity to take a retro on the whole month, but just in terms of just a snapshot.
So this is from Check That, which is the new tool that we're moving on to from Scrunch.
Everything looks fine.
Everything looks fine.
It's good here.
Still holding steady at that top position and up from 39% over the last 30 days.
So all still trending in the right direction.
For CS101, again, trending in the right direction.
The average position for non-branded search has also improved over the last month from 22 to 12.
And impressions are scaling up as well.
So that's, again, all good story of growth there.
And engagement has also been trending up too, which is great to see.
The top landing pages are consistently hitting 65 to 80%, which is a really high in engagement rate.
So it's great to see that climbing as well.
I think the one call out that I think I would like to look into more is I noticed over the last two weeks that the LLM referral traffic has been softening, I think not drastically.
But enough that I would like to dig into it a little bit more for diagnostics.
I think there's a couple things to look at.
I'm going to just cross-reference the citation data with the referral data to see kind of what pages are losing referral traffic.
Because we've seen sometimes it's just a refinement where there's really low-value pages that are losing traffic.
So it's less low-quality traffic, but it hasn't actually kind of altered the value, I would say.
I'd also like to look at the CVE pages specifically to see how they're affecting LLM referrals.

And then the last thing, I think I would like to just do a review of the prompts we're tracking to make sure that, you know, that visibility number we're seeing, the citation number, that it's an accurate reflection of, I think, the audience and the questions that you care most about.
Obviously, Obviously, it's
Not like a direct connection, but I think we rely on this to give us that picture of performance.
So I want to make sure that there's nothing that we're missing there.
So I think just at this age of diagnostics where I'd like to dig in a little bit more and see if we can diagnose what's happening there.
And I'll follow up with the full report next week and some more information there.
Yeah, I think that should be helping.
So I would also like to understand which directory specifically, it is something CS101 or CVE, where we are seeing a drop in LLM traffic.
And also if it is something on CS101, we'd like to know what are those pages that is losing the traffic, LLM traffic.
Yeah, exactly.
So I'll see that's where I'll start to see what landing pages are losing the LLM traffic.
So we can get a sense of what the value is there and kind of that I think.
And I could say it's only specifically.
can
On ChatGPT, mainly?
Yes, yes.
ChatGPT and Perplexity have both declined.

But I think, yeah, ChatGPT, because that's obviously been most of the referral traffic, is the one that's sort of the one that I would like to dig into the most since it accounts for such a large percentage of it.
And so the newer platforms are growing.
They're continuing to grow from a smaller base.
So I think it'll just be, I think checking, I think me doing more diagnostics on which pages are losing the traffic will give us a better sense of what's happening here.
So I'll dig into that for the reporting for next week and come back with a more, hopefully, like a diagnostic of what's going on, and then also suggestions for what we can do to optimize.
Yeah.
Okay, bye.
All right.
I think that was it on my end.
Is there anything else you wanted to touch on?
Are base on today?
I think no.
So for whatever series that has been drafted, that has been published, we will wait for the revised list for the year 2024 and 2025, which has a high volume and we have missed out.
Yeah.
So that should hopefully have that this week so that we can execute on it starting next week.
Yeah.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Okay.
Well, that was it from me.
I will, as soon as I get those comp examples for the threat actor database, I will drop those in Slack for you all to take a look at.
Yeah, sure.
Okay.
All right.
Well, thank you.
Thank you both and have a good day.
Yeah.
Have a nice day.
Bye.
Thank you.
Bye-bye.
Bye.
Bye.

default_summary.markdown_formatted:
## Meeting Purpose

[Weekly sync on content strategy, performance, and operational updates.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=62.0)

## Key Takeaways

  - [**LLM traffic is softening,** driven by declines from ChatGPT and Perplexity. GrowthX will diagnose the cause (e.g., low-value pages) and report back next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [**Content refresh strategy is on hold** pending internal SentinelOne review. GrowthX will build the technical pipeline in parallel to avoid delays.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
  - [**The 2024–25 CVE expansion list is being revised** by a technical SEO expert to correct for missing SEMrush search volume data, ensuring the new list is comprehensive.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)
  - [**Check That tool access is blocked** by a known bug preventing login. GrowthX is escalating the issue to its tech team for a fix.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)

## Topics

### Performance & Diagnostics

  - [**CS101 Performance:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [Non-branded search position improved from 22 → 12.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
      - [Impressions and engagement are scaling; top pages show 65–80% engagement.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=670.0)
  - [**LLM Referral Traffic Softening:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
      - [**Problem:** Referral traffic from ChatGPT and Perplexity has declined over the last two weeks.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
      - [**GrowthX Diagnostic Plan:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
          - [Cross-reference citation data with referral data to identify which pages are losing traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
          - [Analyze CVE pages' specific impact on LLM referrals.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
          - [Review tracked prompts to ensure they accurately reflect SentinelOne's target audience.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
      - [**SentinelOne Request:** Identify the specific directory (CS101 vs. CVE) and pages losing traffic.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=807.0)

### Content Strategy & Pipeline

  - [**Content Refresh Strategy:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=127.0)
      - [**GrowthX Proposal:** A separate pipeline to improve existing content.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=188.0)
          - [**Goal:** Boost ranking for articles stuck on pages 2–3.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=215.0)
          - [**Method:** Update and deepen context while preserving current rankings.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=207.0)
          - [**Process:** Calibrate the approach with a 2-article test.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=277.0)
      - [**Decision:** On hold for internal SentinelOne review (Mansi, Karthi, Ankit).](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
  - [**2024–25 CVE Expansion List:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=68.0)
      - [**Problem:** SEMrush data was missing search volume, creating an incomplete list.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
      - [**Solution:** A technical SEO expert is revising the list to ensure it is comprehensive.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=71.0)
      - [**Action:** Execution on the new list will begin next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=904.0)
  - [**CS101 Content Pipeline:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=99.0)
      - [**Status:** Healthy backlog of \~30 topics in production.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=99.0)
      - [**Action:** SentinelOne to review keyword recommendations for future planning.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
  - [**Threat Actor Database:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [**Action:** GrowthX to share competitor examples ("comps") on Slack today.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [**Decision:** SentinelOne to discuss the project's internal appetite after reviewing the comps.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)

### Tooling & Admin

  - [**Check That Tool Access:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=533.0)
      - [**Problem:** Karthi was blocked by a known bug ("couldn't find your account") during login.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
      - [**Action:** GrowthX to escalate the issue to its tech team for a fix.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
      - [**Access Request:** Mahendra also requires access.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=568.0)
  - [**GA4 Event Mapping:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Need:** A full mapping of GA4 events is required to interpret data from the new custom report.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)
      - [**Action:** Ankit (SentinelOne) will provide the mapping.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)

## Next Steps

  - [**GrowthX:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=931.0)
      - [Share Threat Actor Database comps on Slack (today).](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=425.0)
      - [Escalate Check That login bug to tech team.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=586.0)
      - [Add Mahendra to Check That.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=568.0)
      - [Diagnose LLM traffic decline and report findings next week.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=706.0)
  - [**SentinelOne:**](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
      - [Review content refresh strategy internally and provide feedback by tomorrow.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=318.0)
      - [Review CS101 keyword recommendations.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=378.0)
      - [Provide the full GA4 event mapping.](https://fathom.video/share/Sr7X6EdveZ7ecjnfnxdAiJTWzwvWgorr?tab=summary&timestamp=487.0)

action_items.description: Confirm refresh approach w/ Ankit; update Bailey by Feb 26
Build separate refresh pipeline; prep artifacts for calibration
Share CS101 keyword feedback w/ Bailey
Post Threat Actor Database comp examples in Slack for Karthi/Mansi
Send GA4 event mapping to Bailey
Fix Karthipriya's Check That invite; update Karthipriya on Slack
Diagnose LLM referral drop; cross-ref citations/referrals, CVE impact, prompt relevance; report findings/recs next week
